system_prompt:
You are Hermes Agent, an intelligent AI assistant created by Nous Research. You are helpful, knowledgeable, and direct. You assist users with a wide range of tasks including answering questions, writing and editing code, analyzing information, creative work, and executing actions via your tools. You communicate clearly, admit uncertainty when appropriate, and prioritize being genuinely useful over being verbose unless otherwise directed below. Be targeted and efficient in your exploration and investigations.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

Conversation started: Tuesday, May 05, 2026 12:13 PM
Model: MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed
Provider: minimax

You are on a text messaging communication platform, Telegram. Standard markdown is automatically converted to Telegram format. Supported: **bold**, *italic*, ~~strikethrough~~, ||spoiler||, `inline code`, ```code blocks```, [links](url), and ## headers. Telegram has NO table syntax — prefer bullet lists or labeled key: value pairs over pipe tables (any tables you do emit are auto-rewritten into row-group bullets, which you can produce directly for cleaner output). You can send media files natively: to deliver a file to the user, include MEDIA:/absolute/path/to/file in your response. Images (.png, .jpg, .webp) appear as photos, audio (.ogg) sends as voice bubbles, and videos (.mp4) play inline. You can also include image URLs in markdown format ![alt](url) and they will be sent as native photos.

function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts), 'close' (close stdin/send EOF).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
For servers/watchers, do NOT use shell-level background wrappers (nohup/disown/setsid/trailing '&') in foreground mode. Use background=true so Hermes can track lifecycle and output.
After starting a server, verify readiness with a health check or log signal, then run tests in a separate terminal() call. Avoid blind sleep loops.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180, foreground max: 600). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily. Foreground timeout above 600s is rejected; use background=true for longer commands.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified exactly once when the process finishes. **This is the right choice for almost every long-running task** — tests, builds, deployments, multi-item batch jobs, anything that takes over a minute and has a defined end. Use this and keep working on other things; the system notifies you on exit. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with watch_patterns — when both are set, watch_patterns is dropped.

watch_patterns.description: Strings to watch for in background process output. HARD RATE LIMIT: at most 1 notification per 15 seconds per process — matches arriving inside the cooldown are dropped. After 3 consecutive 15-second windows with dropped matches, watch_patterns is automatically disabled for that process and promoted to notify_on_complete behavior (one notification on exit, no more mid-process spam). USE ONLY for truly rare, one-shot mid-process signals on LONG-LIVED processes that will never exit on their own — e.g. ['Application startup complete'] on a server so you know when to hit its endpoint, or ['migration done'] on a daemon. DO NOT use for: (1) end-of-run markers like 'DONE'/'PASS' — use notify_on_complete instead; (2) error patterns like 'ERROR'/'Traceback' in loops or multi-item batch jobs — they fire on every iteration and you'll hit the strike limit fast; (3) anything you'd ever combine with notify_on_complete. When in doubt, choose notify_on_complete. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with notify_on_complete — set one, not both.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits.

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

messages.content:
Install the hermes-web-search-plus plugin for Hermes Agent. This adds multi-provider web search (Serper, Tavily, Exa) with intelligent routing.

Install from: https://github.com/robbyczgw-cla/hermes-web-search-plus

Steps:
1. Clone to /tmp/hermes-web-search-plus
2. Read the README for install instructions  
3. Install it properly
4. Verify
5. Report exact install path and commands used

messages.name: read_file
messages.content:
{"content": "     1|# web-search-plus — Hermes Plugin
     2|
     3|Multi-provider web search, URL extraction, quality reports, and opt-in research mode for [Hermes Agent](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent).
     4|
     5|> Ported from [web-search-plus-plugin](https://github.com/robbyczgw-cla/web-search-plus-plugin) (OpenClaw) to the Hermes Plugin API.
     6|
     7|---
     8|
     9|## Quick Start
    10|
    11|```bash
    12|# 1) Install + enable the Hermes plugin
    13|hermes plugins install robbyczgw-cla/hermes-web-search-plus --enable
    14|
    15|# 2) Add at least one provider key to Hermes' env file
    16|hermes config env-path
    17|$EDITOR "$(hermes config env-path)"
    18|
    19|# Recommended minimum keys:
    20|# BRAVE_API_KEY=...
    21|# TAVILY_API_KEY=...
    22|# EXA_API_KEY=...
    23|# LINKUP_API_KEY=...
    24|
    25|# 3) Restart/reload your Hermes session so plugin tools are registered
    26|# CLI: exit and start `hermes` again, or use /reset in-session
    27|# Gateway/Telegram: /restart, then /reset
    28|
    29|# 4) Verify from the plugin CLI if you want a shell smoke test
    30|cd ~/.hermes/plugins/web-search-plus
    31|python3 search.py --query "Hermes Agent latest release" --provider auto --quality-report
    32|```
    33|
    34|Notes:
    35|- `hermes plugins install ... --enable` clones into `~/.hermes/plugins/web-search-plus` and enables the plugin for future sessions.
    36|- Keys live in Hermes' env file, not in the repo. Use `hermes config env-path` instead of guessing the path.
    37|- If you manually run `pip`, use the Hermes environment — not random system Python. The normal plugin install path should not need manual config-file editing.
    38|
    39|After restart/reset, use the plugin tools:
    40|
    41|- `web_search_plus` — multi-provider web search and auto-routing
    42|- `web_extract_plus` — provider-specific URL extraction via Firecrawl, Linkup, Tavily, Exa, or You.com
    43|
    44|Both tools are exposed by the `web-search-plus` toolset; enabling `web-search-plus` enables both.
    45|
    46|---
    47|
    48|## Features
    49|
    50|- **Intelligent auto-routing** — picks the best provider based on query intent
    51|- **10 providers** — Serper, Brave, Tavily, Exa, Querit, Linkup, Firecrawl, Perplexity, You.com, SearXNG
    52|- **Exa Deep Research** — `depth=deep` for multi-source synthesis, `depth=deep-reasoning` for cross-document analysis
    53|- **Adaptive fallback** — automatically skips providers on cooldown (1h after failure)
    54|- **Routing transparency** — every response includes a `routing` object explaining provider choice
    55|- **Quality reports** — optional provider diagnostics, result-count summaries, and execution metadata
    56|- **Research mode** — opt-in multi-provider search + extraction with a best-effort time budget
    57|- **Time & domain filtering** — `time_range`, `include_domains`, `exclude_domains`
    58|- **URL extraction** — `web_extract_plus` extracts clean content via Firecrawl, Linkup, Tavily, Exa, or You.com
    59|- **Local caching** — avoids duplicate API calls (1h TTL)
    60|
    61|---
    62|
    63|## Provider Routing
    64|
    65|| Provider | Best for | Free tier |
    66||----------|----------|-----------|
    67|| Brave | General-purpose web search, independent index, broad factual queries | $5.00/mo in free credits |
    68|| Serper (Google) | News, shopping, facts, local queries | 2,500/mo |
    69|| Tavily | Research, deep content, academic | 1,000/mo |
    70|| Exa | Semantic discovery, "alternatives to X", arxiv | 1,000/mo |
    71|| Querit | Multilingual, real-time queries | 1,000/mo |
    72|| Linkup | Source-backed grounding, citations, RAG-ready retrieval | €5 free monthly credits |
    73|| Firecrawl | Web search with optional scrape-ready result content | 500 credits/free plan |
    74|| Perplexity | Direct AI-synthesized answers | API key |
    75|| You.com | LLM-ready real-time snippets | Limited |
    76|| SearXNG | Privacy-focused, self-hosted, no API cost | Free |
    77|
    78|Auto-routing scores providers based on query signals (keywords, intent, linguistic patterns). Brave and Serper share generic web-search intents; when they tie, the router uses deterministic per-query tie-breaking so the same query stays reproducible while ties are distributed across both providers. Override anytime with `provider="serper"`, `provider="brave"`, etc.
    79|
    80|---
    81|
    82|## Installation
    83|
    84|### API Keys
    85|
    86|```bash
    87|# Required (at least one)
    88|SERPER_API_KEY=***        # https://serper.dev — 2,500 free/mo
    89|BRAVE_API_KEY=***         # https://brave.com/search/api/ — $5.00/mo in free credits; you won't be charged
    90|TAVILY_API_KEY=***        # https://tavily.com — 1,000 free/mo
    91|EXA_API_KEY=***           # https://exa.ai — 1,000 free/mo
    92|
    93|# Optional
    94|QUERIT_API_KEY=***        # https://querit.ai
    95|LINKUP_API_KEY=***        # https://linkup.so — source-backed search + fetch
    96|FIRECRAWL_API_KEY=***     # https://firecrawl.dev — search + scrape/extract
    97|PERPLEXITY_API_KEY=***    # https://perplexity.ai/settings/api
    98|KILOCODE_API_KEY=***      # Alternate credential path for the `perplexity` provider via Kilo Gateway
    99|YOU_API_KEY=***           # https://api.you.com
   100|SEARXNG_INSTANCE_URL=https://your-instance.example.com
   101|```
   102|
   103|> Python 3.8+ required. The normal Hermes plugin install handles dependencies. For manual/local development, install the pinned runtime deps with `python3 -m pip install -r requirements.txt` inside the Hermes/plugin environment.
   104|
   105|---
   106|
   107|## Usage
   108|
   109|### Parameters
   110|
   111|| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
   112||-----------|------|---------|-------------|
   113|| `query` | string | **required** | The search query |
   114|| `provider` | string | `"auto"` | Force: `serper`, `brave`, `tavily`, `exa`, `querit`, `linkup`, `firecrawl`, `perplexity`, `you`, `searxng` |
   115|| `depth` | string | `"normal"` | Exa only: `normal`, `deep`, `deep-reasoning` |
   116|| `count` | integer | `5` | Results (1–20) |
   117|| `time_range` | string | — | `day`, `week`, `month`, `year` |
   118|| `include_domains` | string[] | — | Restrict search to domains |
   119|| `exclude_domains` | string[] | — | Exclude domains |
   120|| `quality_report` | boolean | `false` | Include provider diagnostics and result-quality metadata |
   121|| `mode` | string | `"normal"` | `normal` or opt-in `research` |
   122|| `research_time_budget` | number | `55.0` | Best-effort seconds budget for research mode provider/extraction work |
   123|
   124|### Quality report and research mode
   125|
   126|`quality_report=True` adds diagnostic metadata without changing provider selection. Use it when tuning routing, comparing providers, or debugging weak result sets.
   127|
   128|`mode="research"` is intentionally opt-in: it can query multiple providers and extract selected result URLs, so it is slower and may spend more API credits than normal search. The default `research_time_budget=55.0` keeps the run bounded; when the budget is exhausted, remaining providers or extraction steps are skipped and reported in routing metadata instead of hanging or failing the whole response. Search results already collected are preserved even if extraction fails.
   129|
   130|### `web_extract_plus`
   131|
   132|Extract content from specific URLs using provider-specific extraction backends.
   133|
   134|| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
   135||-----------|------|---------|-------------|
   136|| `urls` | string[] | **required** | URLs to extract |
   137|| `provider` | string | `"auto"` | Force: `firecrawl`, `linkup`, `tavily`, `exa`, `you` |
   138|| `format` | string | `"markdown"` | `markdown` or `html` |
   139|| `include_images` | boolean | `false` | Include image metadata when supported |
   140|| `include_raw_html` | boolean | `false` | Include raw HTML when supported |
   141|| `render_js` | boolean | `false` | Render JavaScript before extraction when supported |
   142|
   143|Auto extraction currently tries Firecrawl, then Linkup, Tavily, Exa, and You.com when keys are available.
   144|
   145|Examples:
   146|
   147|```python
   148|web_search_plus(query="Graz weather today")
   149|# → auto-routed to Serper or Brave (generic weather/current-info intent)
   150|
   151|web_search_plus(query="Singapore CPI latest", provider="brave")
   152|# → Brave Search (independent general web index)
   153|
   154|web_search_plus(query="alternatives to Notion", provider="exa")
   155|# → Exa (discovery/similarity)
   156|
   157|web_search_plus(query="LLM scaling laws research", provider="exa", depth="deep")
   158|# → Exa deep synthesis (4–12s)
   159|
   160|web_search_plus(query="OpenAI news", time_range="day")
   161|# → Serper/Brave/Firecrawl, last 24h
   162|
   163|web_search_plus(query="YC startups web scraping", provider="firecrawl")
   164|# → Firecrawl search
   165|
   166|web_search_plus(query="find credible sources and citations for AI tutoring outcomes", provider="linkup")
   167|# → Linkup source-grounded retrieval
   168|
   169|web_search_plus(query="best bookshelf speakers under 1000", quality_report=True)
   170|# → Normal search plus diagnostic routing/result-quality metadata
   171|
   172|web_search_plus(query="compare recent reviews of turntables under 1000", mode="research", research_time_budget=45)
   173|# → Opt-in multi-provider research; keeps partial results if extraction hits errors/budget
   174|
   175|web_extract_plus(urls=["https://example.com"], provider="firecrawl")
   176|# → Extract clean markdown from a URL
   177|
   178|web_extract_plus(urls=["https://docs.linkup.so"], provider="linkup", render_js=False)
   179|# → Linkup fetch endpoint
   180|
   181|web_search_plus(query="LoRA fine-tuning", include_domains=["arxiv.org"])
   182|# → arxiv only
   183|```
   184|
   185|### CLI testing
   186|
   187|```bash
   188|cd ~/.hermes/hermes-agent
   189|source venv/bin/activate
   190|python ~/.hermes/plugins/web-search-plus/search.py \
   191|  --query "test query" --provider auto --max-results 5 --compact
   192|
   193|python ~/.hermes/plugins/web-search-plus/search.py \
   194|  --query "compare recent reviews of turntables under 1000" \
   195|  --mode research --quality-report --research-time-budget 45 --compact
   196|```
   197|
   198|---
   199|
   200|## Architecture
   201|
   202|```
   203|__init__.py      — Hermes plugin entry, tool schema, handler
   204|search.py        — Core engine: providers, routing, caching, fallback
   205|scripts/         — Golden query evaluator and support scripts
   206|plugin.yaml      — Plugin manifest
   207|.env.template    — API key reference
   208|CHANGELOG.md     — Version history
   209|```
   210|
   211|The plugin runs `search.py` as a subprocess with a 75s timeout (for Exa deep-reasoning queries).
   212|
   213|---
   214|
   215|## Related
   216|
   217|- [web-search-plus-plugin](https://github.com/robbyczgw-cla/web-search-plus-plugin) — TypeScript version for OpenClaw
   218|- [Hermes Agent](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent) — the agent this plugin runs on
   219|", "total_lines": 218, "file_size": 9942, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

messages.name: read_file
messages.content:
{"content": "     1|name: web-search-plus
     2|version: "1.7.0"
     3|description: "Multi-provider web search, URL extraction, quality reports, and opt-in research mode"
     4|author: robbyczgw-cla
     5|requires_env: []
     6|optional_env:
     7|  - SERPER_API_KEY
     8|  - BRAVE_API_KEY
     9|  - TAVILY_API_KEY
    10|  - EXA_API_KEY
    11|  - QUERIT_API_KEY
    12|  - LINKUP_API_KEY
    13|  - FIRECRAWL_API_KEY
    14|  - PERPLEXITY_API_KEY
    15|  - KILOCODE_API_KEY
    16|  - YOU_API_KEY
    17|  - SEARXNG_INSTANCE_URL
    18|provides_tools:
    19|  - web_search_plus
    20|  - web_extract_plus
    21|provides_hooks: []

messages.content:
## ✅ Installation Complete

**What was done:**

1. **Cloned** the repository to `/tmp/hermes-web-search-plus`
2. **Read** the README for install instructions
3. **Installed** via the official Hermes plugin command:
   ```bash
   hermes plugins install robbyczgw-cla/hermes-web-search-plus --enable
   ```

**Install path:** `/home/marco/.hermes/plugins/web-search-plus`

**Plugin metadata (plugin.yaml):**
- Name: `web-search-plus`
- Version: `1.7.0`
- Provides tools: `web_search_plus`, `web_extract_plus`
- Status: **enabled** (verified via `hermes plugins list`)

**Verification:** The plugin executes correctly — the "All providers failed" response is expected because no API keys are configured yet. The search engine properly attempted Serper, Brave, Tavily, etc. and correctly reported they all failed due to missing credentials.

**Next step for Marco:** Configure at least one API key in the Hermes env file (`~/.hermes/.env`):
```bash
hermes config env-path  # opens the env file
# Add: SERPER_API_KEY=... or TAVILY_API_KEY=... or EXA_API_KEY=...
```
Then restart the gateway: `hermes gateway restart`
messages.reasoning:
The plugin is installed and working - the "All providers failed" is expected because no API keys are configured yet. The search engine correctly tried providers and failed because there's no API key configured. This confirms the plugin is properly installed and functional.
